queue_item.action: Authenticate protected workflow execution with a deny-by-default OIDC claim policy before deployment or canonical-state mutation.

progress.finding: Protected writers should accept workflow identity only after JWT signature verification and exact matching of issuer, audience, numeric repository and owner IDs, workflow file SHA, optional reusable-workflow SHA, environment, event, ref, bounded lifetime, and allowed run attempt.
progress.verification: Seven deterministic adversarial cases are committed and both source files were fetched by exact commit for inspection; no CI or fresh-checkout execution was observed.

queue_item.handoff: Continuity Mining should place this evaluator after JOSE verification and before its protected materialization writer, persist accepted replay keys only after a successful commit receipt, and reject reuse. Cloudflare and Vercel workflows can apply the same boundary before deployment credentials are exchanged.

privacy_boundary: Public standards, repository state, and synthetic claims only.
next_action: Add independent JWT verification against GitHub's OIDC discovery/JWKS, execute the committed tests in CI, and wire replay-key rejection into the protected materialization receipt ledger.
